zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Breton於1324年出生在英國英格蘭德比夏爾沃爾頓 武朋 特倫特時，她的父親Sir Robert Breton of Walton34歲，母親Mrs. Margaret Breton35歲。她於1354年與John Loudham II在英國諾廷加姆爾洛達哈姆結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1376年在英國諾廷加姆爾洛達哈姆享年52歲。

nameMeanings.name: Breton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Normand, Renald, Adelard, Alphonse, Armand, Gaetan, Laurent, Serge, Adrien, Aime.
French, Breton (mainly Finistère), and English (of Norman origin): ethnic name for a Breton, from Old French breton, oblique case of bret (see Bret ). Compare Lebreton , see also English Brett .

History: In North America, this surname was originally also a secondary surname or dit (‘also called’) name, borne by Jean Élie/Hélie dit (Le) Breton from Ménéac in Morbihan, Brittany (France), who married Jeanne Labbé in Sainte-Famille, Île d'Orléans, QC, in 1669. His descendants also bear the surname Elie .

variants.name: Bret
meanings: French: ethnic name for a Breton, from Old French bret, nominative of breton ‘Breton’ (see Breton ). See also English Brett .

variants.name: Brittain
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Anglo-Norman French Bretagne, Bretaign(e) ‘Brittany’. Some of the modern forms are from the Middle English Brytane, Brittan ‘Breton’; compare Breton .

variants.name: Britton
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Breton , and ethnic name for a Breton, from Old French Breton, the oblique case form of Old French Bret; compare Brittain and Brett .

variants.name: Bretton
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: variant of   Breton  .

English: habitational name from any of the places called from Bretton in Derbyshire and Yorkshire, both of which mean ‘settlement of the Britons’, from Old English Brettas ‘Briton’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Breton was born in 1324, in Walton upon Trent, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Robert Breton of Walton, was 34 and her mother, Mrs. Margaret Breton, was 35. She married John Loudham II in 1354, in Lowdham, Nottinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1376, in Lowdham, Nottinghamshire, England, at the age of 52.

fr.Walton upon Trent, Derbyshire, England: Walton upon Trent, Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel Breton est née en 1324, à Walton upon Trent, Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Robert Breton of Walton, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Margaret Breton, avait 35 ans. Elle a épousé John Loudham II en 1354, à Lowdham, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1376, à Lowdham, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 52 ans.

Sie hat 1354 in Lowdham, Nottinghamshire, England John Loudham II geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1376 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Lowdham, Nottinghamshire, England.

ko.Walton upon Trent, Derbyshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 월턴 업 트렌트
ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Breton(이)가 1324년 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 월턴 업 트렌트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Robert Breton of Walton(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs. Margaret Breton(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 1354년 영국 노틴그함실르 로드함에서 John Loudham II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1376년 영국 노틴그함실르 로드함에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com John Loudham II em 1354, em Lowdham, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. ru.Walton upon Trent, Derbyshire, England: валтон упон трент, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel Breton родилась в 1324 г. в местности валтон упон трент, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir Robert Breton of Walton было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Margaret Breton, 35. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Loudham II в 1354 г. в местности лоудхам, ноттингхамшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1376 г. в местности лоудхам, ноттингхамшир, Англия в возрасте 52 лет.

Se casó con John Loudham II en 1354, en Lowdham, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas.
